warnings: This sheet's grid only spans gridE down to gridA -- grid F (the northernmost row) is NOT part of the tie-beam plan, meaning the tie beam (คานอะเส, typically a ground-level grade beam) doesn't extend to the northernmost bay. Consistent with S-02/S-03's footing plans, which also show no footing at grid1-gridF (only grid2-gridF and grid4-gridF have footings).

Every interior bay is labeled 'โล่ง' (void/open) -- the tie-beam grid forms only the perimeter + one interior spine (RB3 at grid2) and one cross beam (RB2 at gridC and gridB between grid2-grid4), with the bay interiors left open. This is typical for a tie-beam/grade-beam plan on pile foundations where the ground slab is poured separately or left as crawl space -- not fully explained by this sheet alone, may become clearer from S-08 (beam expansion) rebar specs.

CONFLICTING extension depth south of grid A across 3 different structural sheets: S-04 (floor-1 beam plan) shows a real beam ~2.05m south of grid A; S-05 (floor-2 beam plan) shows only ~0.40m; this sheet (S-06, tie beam) shows dashed dimensions of 0.70m and 0.50m for a small extension in the same area. These are NOT being reconciled into a single number -- each is recorded as printed on its own sheet, flagged here for human structural review. It's plausible these represent different real features at slightly different plan depths (e.g. a covered carport roof overhang at floor 1 vs. a small ground-level utility pad at the tie-beam level), but this needs a human to confirm against the architecture drawings rather than an assumption made here.
RB2/RB3 appear to be heavier-duty tie-beam marks than the standard RB1 perimeter beam -- likely correspond to specific rebar specs on S-08 (ขยายคาน คสล.), not yet read at time of writing this file.
